title: Parts and Types of Fruits and Vegetables

senses.definition: safe or suitable for eating

examples.x: He found an edible mushroom in the forest.
He gifted his friend a bouquet of edible flowers, perfect for garnishing salads and drinks.
She decorated her cake with edible glitter for a touch of sparkle.

senses.definition: not capable of being eaten or is not safe for consumption

examples.x: He accidentally took a bite of the inedible fruit and quickly spat it out.
He received a beautifully decorated cake, but the fondant decorations were inedible.
We found an inedible plant in the garden and decided to remove it for safety reasons.

senses.definition: a small, dry fruit that typically contains a single seed and is often surrounded by a hardened outer layer

examples.x: As the wind blew, the achene detached from the plant and floated away, carried by the breeze.
The squirrel buried the achene in the ground, unknowingly aiding in the plant's reproduction.
They planted the achene in the soil, hoping it would grow into a beautiful flower.

senses.definition: a plant seedpod that contains multiple seeds, such as peas or peanuts

examples.x: He discovered a new variety of legume while exploring the rainforest.
My friend studied the nutritional benefits of different legumes for her research project.

senses.definition: a small fruit with a seed inside a hard shell that grows on some trees

examples.x: Almonds are a type of nut that's high in healthy fats and protein.
Peanuts, despite their name, are actually not a true nut but a legume.
She sprinkled chopped almonds, a healthy nut, over her morning oatmeal.
The squirrel was running with a small nut in its mouth.

senses.definition: a one-seeded fruit where the seed is fused with the fruit wall, commonly found in grasses and grains

examples.x: As the harvest season arrived, the farmer gathered caryopses of various legumes.
The child carefully planted caryopses of wildflowers in the garden.
We examined the caryopsis of the rice grain under the microscope, observing its unique structure.

senses.definition: a type of fruit that consists of an outer fleshy layer, a hard inner shell, and a single seed

examples.x: Birds and animals are attracted to drupes.
The market was filled with an assortment of drupes, each displaying its unique shape, size, and color.

senses.definition: a dry, hard covering type of fruit that splits open to release seeds, such as in poppy seeds or okra

examples.x: He crushed the dried capsules of the poppy flowers to extract the tiny seeds for culinary purposes.
She collected capsules from various plants and carefully stored them for future study.
The children collected capsules from the oak trees, playing a game to see whose capsule would burst open first.

senses.definition: a type of fruit that has a hard, pit-like stone (or seed) surrounded by fleshy edible fruit

examples.x: Her favorite among all the stone fruits is the peach.
Her grandmother's garden was full of stone fruit trees.

senses.definition: a fruit with a central core surrounded by a fleshy layer

examples.x: He picked a ripe apple from the tree, enjoying the crispness of the pome.
In her grandmother's garden, there were several pomes, including apples and quinces.

senses.definition: any fruit with a sour taste, such as oranges, limes, and tangerines

examples.x: He squeezed fresh lemon juice into his water, enjoying the tangy taste of the citrus.

senses.definition: a cluster of small individual fruits that develop from separate ovaries within a single flower

examples.x: She enjoyed the juicy sweetness of raspberries, an aggregate fruit.
We gathered elderberries, excited to use the aggregate fruits for making jams and pies.

senses.definition: a fruit formed from the fused ovaries of multiple flowers

examples.x: She enjoyed a delicious smoothie made with a blend of multiple fruits.
The chef incorporated chunks of multiple fruits into a delectable dessert.

senses.definition: a type of fruit where the flesh comes from tissues other than the ovary

examples.x: The children were excited to try the unusual-looking dragon fruit, an exotic accessory fruit.
The farmers grew a diverse range of accessory fruits on their orchard, including grapes and blueberries.

senses.definition: not containing any seeds

examples.x: I couldn't resist the temptation of buying a bag of seedless tangerines.
My son prefers the seedless grapes because they are easy to eat.

senses.definition: having an abundance of leaves or characterized by the presence of leaves

examples.x: He admired the vibrant colors of the leafy plants in his garden.
The restaurant offered a refreshing salad with a mix of leafy greens and crunchy vegetables.

senses.definition: a variety of leafy vegetables that are commonly used as the base for salads

examples.x: My grandfather enjoys growing his own salad greens in his backyard garden.
My son prefers to top his salad greens with cherry tomatoes.

senses.definition: the ball-shaped root of some plants that grows anew every year

examples.x: She discovered a forgotten bulb of garlic in the pantry and decided to use it in her recipe.
The gardener dug a hole and gently placed the bulb of an onion into the soil.
In spring, the tulip bulb sprouted leaves and eventually bloomed into vibrant flowers.
Gardeners plant daffodil bulbs in the fall so they can bloom in early spring.
The onion bulb grew underground and was harvested for cooking.

senses.definition: the main part of a plant that connects the roots to the twigs, leaves, and flowers

examples.x: The scientist examined the stem under a microscope to study its structure and how it conducts nutrients.
After picking the herbs, she removed the stems and used the leaves for cooking.
The gardener trimmed the stem of the rose bush to promote new growth and healthier blooms.
She carefully cut the stems of the flowers before arranging them in a vase to ensure they absorbed water properly.

senses.definition: the underground part of a plant that absorbs water and minerals, sending it to other parts

examples.x: The root of the vegetable was harvested, revealing a crisp and fresh produce ready for cooking.
The herbalist used the root of the herb in the remedy, valuing its medicinal properties.

senses.definition: a swollen, underground stem or root of a plant that stores nutrients

examples.x: He harvested the sweet potato tubers and cooked them in a flavorful stew.
She carefully lifted the ginger tuber from the soil.
The chef used the horseradish tuber to add a kick of flavor to her signature sauce.

senses.definition: having or resembling tubers, which are thickened, fleshy underground stems or roots

examples.x: It was a delight to see the tuberous vines of the morning glory climbing up the garden fence.
The farmer harvested the tuberous roots of the yam plant.

senses.definition: a large long vegetable of the squash family with green skin and white flesh, grown on the ground

examples.x: My mother added diced marrow to a summer salad, providing a refreshing and nutritious element to the mix.
She stuffed the hollowed-out marrow with a flavorful rice and vegetable filling for a healthy crispy snack.

senses.definition: the central part of a fruit that typically contains seeds, like in apples, pears, and pineapples

examples.x: She carefully removed the core of the apple before taking a bite.
The recipe called for the removal of the core from the orange before juicing it.
They used a special tool to effortlessly remove the core of the pear.
After cutting the melon, she discarded the core.
He ate the sweet parts of the peach and tossed the core.

senses.definition: a simple sugar that is commonly used as a sweetener or a source of energy in various food and medical products

examples.x: My mother always keeps a jar of dextrose in my pantry for baking cookies and cakes.

senses.definition: the soft, edible part of a fruit, typically rich in flavor and texture

examples.x: He dipped a spoon into the luscious flesh of the ripe cantaloupe.
He peeled back the skin of the kiwi, revealing its vibrant green flesh dotted with tiny black seeds.

senses.definition: the outer covering or shell of a seed or fruit

examples.x: He carefully removed the hull of the walnut before cracking it open.
She used a knife to scrape the hull off the coconut to reveal the white flesh inside.

senses.definition: the liquid inside fruits and vegetables or the drink that we make from them

examples.x: They mixed the juices of different fruits to create a delicious and colorful blend.

senses.definition: a sweet, liquid substance produced by flowers and used by insects as a source of energy

examples.x: She carefully collected the nectar from the blooming flowers to make a delicious homemade honey.
They watched as the bees buzzed around, sipping the nectar from the colorful blossoms.

senses.definition: a natural substance found in fruits that is used as a thickening agent in food preparation

examples.x: I bought pectin at the store to make my own jelly from fresh fruits.
She added pectin to the strawberry jam to help it thicken.

senses.definition: the outer, protective layer of a fruit that can be removed

examples.x: He carefully removed the peel of the orange and savored the juicy segments.

senses.definition: a tiny hard seed that is found in some fruits such as an apple, peach, etc.

examples.x: He accidentally swallowed a pip while eating the juicy watermelon.
The squirrel buried the hazelnut pip in the ground for safekeeping.

senses.definition: a small, hard seed or stone found inside certain fruits, such as peaches and cherries

examples.x: He enjoyed cracking open the pits of the apricot and extracting the tasty kernel inside.
I accidentally bit into a pit while eating the plum and had to be careful next time.

senses.definition: the spongy tissue found inside the stems and branches of plants

examples.x: She examined the pith under a microscope to study its cellular structure.
The pith of the citrus fruit added a bitter taste to the juice.
We collected samples of pith from different plants to compare their properties.

senses.definition: the soft, fibrous part of a fruit or vegetable

examples.x: He carefully removed the pomegranate seeds, leaving behind the vibrant red pulp.
She squeezed the orange, releasing the refreshing pulp into her glass.

senses.definition: the tough outer covering or skin of a fruit or vegetables

examples.x: As the sun beat down, they relaxed on the beach, sipping on cold beverages with slices of lime rind floating on top.
The bartender garnished the cocktail with a twist of citrus rind.

senses.definition: a small, hard object produced by a fruit or vegetable that can grow into a new one

examples.x: The gardener planted the seeds in rows to ensure they have enough space to grow.
After the fruit was sliced, she carefully removed the seeds before serving it in a salad.
He decided to start his own vegetable garden by ordering organic seeds online.
Each seed contains the potential to grow into a new plant, making gardening a rewarding experience.
The farmer saved the best seeds from his harvest to use for planting in the next season.

senses.definition: any of the inner parts of a fruit such as an orange, lemon, etc. that can be easily separated

examples.x: He enjoyed biting into the juicy segments of the watermelon on a hot summer day.
They divided the grapefruit into equal segments to share among the group.

senses.definition: the outer layer that covers a fruit, seed, or vegetable
the slender, elongated part of a plant that supports leaves, flowers, or fruits

examples.x: As she prepared the celery for a salad, she trimmed the leafy stalks and discarded the tougher parts.
They picked the juicy tomatoes, gently twisting the stalks to separate them from the vine.

senses.definition: the hard seed or pit found inside certain fruits like peaches and cherries

examples.x: The baker skillfully removed the stone from the apricot, preparing it for a delicious pie.
They carefully extracted the stone from the nectarine, savoring the velvety flesh around it.

senses.definition: the flavorful outer layer of citrus fruit peel used to add tangy taste to dishes
a small, round nut with a hard shell, known for its rich flavor and used in cooking and snacks

examples.x: I found a cob of hazelnuts lying on the ground and eagerly started shelling them.

senses.definition: the central cylindrical part of the corn ear that is covered in tightly packed kernelsn

examples.x: The chickens pecked at the corncob, eagerly devouring the remaining bits of corn.
We roasted the corncobs on the campfire, savoring the smoky flavor.

senses.definition: a small opening or indentation on the surface of certain fruits or vegetables, often associated with the attachment point or reproductive structures, such as in potatoes, pineapples, and beans

examples.x: She examined the potato closely, noticing the small eye where a new sprout would eventually emerge.

senses.definition: a small flower or cluster of flowers that form the flowering head of vegetables like broccoli, cauliflower, and Brussels sprouts, commonly used in culinary applications

examples.x: He picked off the tiny florets from the cauliflower head to use in his salad.
The farmer harvested the fresh broccoli florets from the garden for market.

senses.definition: the edible leafy greens or uppermost part of certain plants, used in culinary applications

examples.x: He carefully arranged the tops of the radishes on a platter, turning them into an eye-catching centerpiece for the table.
I chopped the beet tops and tossed them into a stir-fry, creating a colorful and nutritious meal.
He chopped the beet tops and sautéed them with garlic for a nutritious side dish.
The salad was garnished with fresh herb tops, adding a burst of flavor and color to the dish.

senses.definition: the tissue within a seed that provides nourishment to the developing embryo

examples.x: The researcher studied the endosperm of various seeds, aiming to understand its role in plant development and nutrition.
With a knife, he separated the endosperm from the seed, revealing its starchy and nutrient-rich composition.

senses.definition: the middle layer of a fruit found between the outer skin and the inner core or seed, typically fleshy in texture

examples.x: The market vendor displayed a variety of tropical fruits, each with its own unique-colored mesocarp.

senses.definition: the protective outer covering of a seed

examples.x: I carefully removed the seed coat of the pumpkin seed before roasting it for a tasty snack.
They observed how the seed coat of the coconut was tough and fibrous, helping to shield the inner seed.

senses.definition: the outermost layer or skin of a fruit

examples.x: As I cut into the exocarp of the pineapple, its tropical aroma filled the room, making my mouth water.
They carefully removed the exocarp of the pomegranate, revealing the clusters of vibrant red arils inside.

senses.definition: all the layers of tissue that make up the wall of a fruit

examples.x: As the peach ripened, its pericarp softened and its sweet aroma filled the room.
The squirrel nibbled on the pericarp of the hazelnut, trying to access the tasty kernel hidden inside.

senses.definition: (of plant or fruit tissue) soft, juicy, and succulent, often with a high water content

examples.x: She plucked a fleshy strawberry from the garden and enjoyed its juicy sweetness.
The chef prepared a fleshy avocado salad, combining its creamy texture with tangy dressing.
The children enjoyed the fleshy pulp of the mango, their faces smeared with its sweet and sticky juice.

senses.definition: preserved or enhanced in flavor and appearance through the application of a sugary syrup or glaze
(of fruits or vegetables) excessively ripe or beyond the point of optimal freshness
having had the pits or stones removed

examples.x: He noticed the pitted olives in the salad and picked them out.

senses.definition: (of fruit or crop) fully developed and ready for consumption

examples.x: You can enjoy the natural sweetness of a ripe peach by biting into it and letting the juice drip down your chin.
She picked a ripe avocado that yielded slightly to gentle pressure.
The ripe strawberries were bursting with juicy sweetness.
He waited until the bananas were ripe with brown speckles before making banana bread.
The tomatoes were perfectly ripe, with a vibrant red color and firm texture.

senses.definition: typical or customary for a specific time of year

examples.x: They enjoyed seasonal fruits and vegetables from the local farmer's market.
The clothing store stocked up on seasonal winter coats and scarves.
She decorated her home with seasonal decorations for Halloween.
They served seasonal pumpkin spice lattes at the coffee shop in the fall.
The resort offered seasonal discounts for summer vacation packages.
She put up seasonal decorations to celebrate the holiday season.
His seasonal allergies flare up every spring when the pollen count rises.
They enjoy seasonal activities, such as skiing in the winter and hiking in the summer.
Seasonal changes in weather influence the types of clothing available in stores.

senses.definition: (particularly of food) having been dried in the sun

examples.x: I discovered a new recipe that called for sun-dried cranberries.
I used sun-dried herbs to enhance the taste of my homemade pasta sauce.
